priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has suspended his current proxy campaign to call a special meeting of shareholders due to a judge's last-minute recusal, which has disrupted the timeline for the strategic process. Neugebauer's campaign had earned the backing of over 70% of votes cast and two leading proxy advisory firms, and he remains confident in Fermi's ability to secure a tenant group and achieve long-term success. Neugebauer will continue to push for good governance through the judicial process and monitor the board's execution on Project Matador to ensure maximum value for stakeholders.

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., disclosed that the company has dropped its lawsuit against him, abandoning its effort to validate a 70% supermajority bylaw amendment, after a Texas court ordered the company to produce documents and testimony explaining the rationale for the bylaw. Neugebauer's claims challenging the validity of the bylaw remain alive and he will continue to pursue the truth through discovery and depositions, which he believes is necessary for good governance and to lower the company's cost of capital. The development is significant as leading proxy advisory firms Glass Lewis and Egan-Jones have recommended that shareholders consent to th

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi America, delivered an investor presentation on June 30, 2026, expressing concerns about the company's contracts, including the EPC contract and gas contract, which he believes could be either assets or liabilities. Neugebauer emphasized the importance of having the right team to steward these contracts and ensure the company's success, and he advocated for a dual-track process and evidence-based decision-making in evaluating potential tenants. Neugebauer also stated that he and his team are not trying to come back to the company, but rather want to ensure that the board prioritizes the interests of shareholders.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and other participants, including Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and Melissa A. Neugebauer 2020 Trust, have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with soliciting agent designations for a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The filing discloses that Mr. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi Inc.'s common stock, while the other participants do not beneficially own any shares. The participants are soliciting support for calling a special meeting of shareholders to be held as promptly as practicable.

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, the co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., is hosting a Town Hall webinar to answer questions from analysts and provide clarity and transparency to shareholders, after receiving support from two leading proxy advisory firms, Glass Lewis and Egan-Jones, to call a special meeting of shareholders. The webinar is scheduled for June 30, 2026, and Neugebauer has invited Fermi leadership, including Governor Rick Perry and Co-President Jacobo Ortiz, to join and provide answers, although they have so far declined. Neugebauer and affiliated entities have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with the solicitation of agent designations for calling a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock, and is advising all shareholders to read the definitive proxy statement and other proxy materials because they contain important information. priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has announced that the Texas Business Court granted his motion for expedited discovery, requiring Fermi to provide documentation and testimony regarding its recent actions, including the imposition of a 70% supermajority voting requirement. This development is a significant win for Neugebauer and Fermi's shareholders, who have been seeking greater accountability and transparency from the company's board. Neugebauer will also host a Town Hall webinar with analysts on June 30 to address questions and provide updates on his efforts to restore "good governance" to Fermi.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi America, Inc., has proposed a town hall webinar for June 30th where he, Governor Rick Perry, and other parties will answer questions from analysts and journalists about the company's past, present, and future. Neugebauer is seeking to give shareholders a say in the company's direction, particularly regarding Project Matador, a 7,500-acre private power campus in Texas, and whether to pursue a strategic dual-track process to maximize shareholder value. The proposed webinar comes after Governor Perry and the board gave themselves a 70% supermajority vote threshold, making it difficult for shareholders to change course.

priority.summary: Fermi Inc is urging shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card in response to former CEO Toby Neugebauer's consent solicitation to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders, citing Neugebauer's pattern of false claims and inaccurate statements. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the Board and leadership team with his own nominees, which Fermi's Board believes would jeopardize shareholder value and benefit Neugebauer and his affiliates at the expense of other shareholders. The company is highlighting its commercial momentum and progress under new management, and warning shareholders not to sign or return any GREEN Consent Cards sent by Neugebauer.

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, along with other participants, has issued a press release reaffirming his support for John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO of Fermi, citing the need for enhanced accountability and credibility to prevent value destruction by the current Board and management team. Neugebauer claims that the current Board has failed to replace nine key senior executives who departed Fermi after his termination and has ignored proposals from large companies that could offer greater value to shareholders. The release also highlights Neugebauer's intent to continue his campaign to seat a new, strong, independent Board and restore Fermi's position as a pro-shareholder REIT.

priority.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc is mailing a Consent Revocation Statement to shareholders in response to a consent solicitation by former CEO Toby Neugebauer, who is seeking to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders. The company's Board of Directors urges shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card to protect their investment and support the company's continued execution of its Fermi 2.0 strategy. Neugebauer, who was terminated for cause, currently does not have the required support to call a Special Meeting, and the company emphasizes that it is making progress on its projects, including Project Matador, and is focused on maximizing long-term shareholder value.

related.title: Exclusive: Nvidia taps new corporate comms chief
related.summary: Nvidia has hired Anna Soellner, former VP of communications at Reddit, as its new head of corporate communications. This move is significant as Nvidia is at the forefront of the AI boom, and Soellner will be crucial in shaping its public narrative amidst high scrutiny from various stakeholders. Her experience at Reddit managing complex issues and transitioning the platform to a mainstream entity will be valuable in addressing public unease surrounding rapid AI advancements. related.summary: Nvidia's stock climbed over 1% after the company refuted a report from SemiAnalysis about a significant delay in its Kyber NVL144 rack-scale system. Nvidia stated that its AI "roadmap is intact," despite claims of manufacturing issues pushing back the Kyber system by over a year to 2028.
